page: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor architecture operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU configuration provides 64 vCPUs with a single thread per core, supported by 64 KB of L1D cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 128 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64pls_v6 demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance and memory throughput. Multi-core stress-ng tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core metrics all place in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block reads reaching 7275.51 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads reaching 465.6 GB/sec, both scoring in the top tier. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 10%. Conversely, single-core performance metrics, such as single-core stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D64pls_v6 offers high compute density with its 64 dedicated physical ARM cores, making it highly economical for parallelized workloads. However, the low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a tradeoff, rendering it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or massive datasets. It is best suited for compute-optimized workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded data decompression, high-throughput static web serving, and highly parallelized database operations that fit within the 128.0 GB memory envelope.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM-based server from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it is configured with 128.0 GB of memory, providing a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server lacks local storage and GPUs but is premium storage capable. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high-throughput database and static web serving capabilities, though single-core performance remains average. This compute-optimized profile offers excellent resource density for highly parallelized tasks. It is highly suited for software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and high-concurrency web serving workloads that do not require massive memory allocations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is a compute-optimized ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pls_v6 is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated cores and 128 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput parallel compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 compute platform delivering exceptional multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallelized workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores operating at 3.4 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance
No bundled local storage or GPUs, with premium storage capability
Optimized for software compilation, static web serving, and parallel processing
